title: The One With Barry and Mindy's Wedding

expressions.dialogue: Ross: Well, hey. You're an actor, I say you just **suck it up** and do it.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 힘든 상황을 감수하고 행동하라는 직접적인 격려. 가까운 사이에서는 자연스럽지만, 상대의 감정을 무시하는 것처럼 들릴 수 있어 관계와 상황을 봐 가며 써야 함.
expressions.example: 발표 전에 잔뜩 긴장해서 꾸물거리는 친구에게 → "Look, we all get nervous. Just suck it up and get on stage."

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: So suck it up man, it's a job, it's money.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: This sucks, I was just up by that much!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh, yeah, sure. I just hope you, hope you don't accidentally suck it up through your nose and choke on it.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: (looks at Ross) / Ross: **Over my dead body!** / Joey: (looks at Chandler) / Chandler: And I'll be using his dead body as a shield.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 절대 안 돼, 내가 죽기 전엔 그런 일 없다
expressions.nuance: 극단적인 거부감을 유머러스하게 표현하는 관용구. 실제 죽음과 무관하며, 장난스러운 상황과 진지한 거부 모두에 씀. Chandler의 즉석 드립처럼 이 표현을 발판으로 농담이 이어지기도 함.
expressions.example: 남동생이 내 차를 파티에 몰고 가겠다고 함 → "You're taking my car to a frat party? Over my dead body."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh no-no, no, I made a promise to myself that the next time I would talk to Ursula would be over my dead body. And that's not happening 'til October 15th, 2032.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: Joey, maybe you're just not used to kissing men, maybe you just **tensed up** a little — maybe that's what you need to work on.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 신체적으로 근육이 경직되거나 심리적으로 긴장하는 것 모두에 씀. 'freeze up'(완전히 얼어버림)보다는 가볍고, 자연스러운 반응으로 묘사할 때 적합.
expressions.example: 처음 하는 운전에서 핸들을 너무 꽉 잡음 → "Try not to tense up — relax your grip on the wheel."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: I can't. It's like this chemical thing, you know. Every time she starts laughing, I just wanna (grimaces and tenses up) pull my arm off just so that I can have something to throw at her.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: Ooh! Someone's wearing the same clothes they had on last night. Someone **get a little action**?
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 친한 친구들 사이에서 쓰는 가벼운 속어. 직접적인 표현보다 완곡하지만, 공식적인 자리나 처음 보는 사람에게는 부적절함.
expressions.example: 친구가 엄청 기분 좋아 보이고 외박을 한 것 같음 → "You seem suspiciously happy this morning. Did you get a little action last night?"

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: But my mom always makes them. It's like a tradition. You get a little piece of turkey on your fork, a little cranberry sauce, and a tot! It's bad enough I can't be with my family because of my disease.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: No, if he doesn't like our cookies, too bad, I am not gonna be blackmailed. Look if worse comes to worse, it gets a little warm, we'll call it a theme party.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay, hold on real quick, hold on a second let me just uh, (sits on the counter and buttons her sweater to show some cleavage) get a little more comfortable here. Wait, now wait a second, this isn't too revealing is it?

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Well, we haven't exactly met — we just **stayed up all night** talking on the internet.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 밤을 새다, 자지 않고 밤 내내 깨어 있다
expressions.nuance: 'pull an all-nighter'가 주로 공부·업무에 쓰인다면, 'stay up all night'은 이유에 상관없이 밤새 깨어 있었다는 사실 자체를 서술할 때 폭넓게 씀.
expressions.example: 연애 초기 설레는 통화가 새벽까지 이어짐 → "We stayed up all night talking — I didn't even realize it was 4 a.m."

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh, it was great! Yeah, I-I walked her home, and it was amazing how much we connected, y'know? Then ah, then she passed out, but then she woke up. Yeah? And we stayed up all night talking, and now we're like totally crazy about each other!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: This sucks! I didn't know I had to stay up all night before I went to this stupid sleep clinic! I'm so tired!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Come on, it's not a big deal! We stayed up all night coming up with a plan so that us dating will not be a problem.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: You know how sometimes I tend to get a little **defensive** and quippy... Well, she totally called me on it. She said, 'Cut it out, get real', and I did.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 피드백이나 지적을 받을 때 반사적으로 자신을 보호하려는 태도. 'quippy'(농담으로 방어)와 자주 짝지어 쓰여 감정을 유머로 회피하는 패턴을 묘사함.
expressions.example: 피드백을 주면 항상 반박부터 하는 동료에 대해 → "Every time I give him feedback, he gets defensive instead of actually listening."

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: She said, '**Cut it out**, get real', and I did.
expressions.speaker: Chandler (인터넷 친구의 말을 인용)
expressions.nuance: 'stop it'보다 강하고 직접적인 표현. 반복되는 행동이나 태도에 진지하게, 또는 약간 짜증스럽게 제동을 걸 때 씀.
expressions.example: 동생이 계속 장난을 침 → "Cut it out — I'm actually trying to concentrate."

occ.speaker: All
occ.text: Oh, hey, don't do that! Cut it out!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: What? Monica, they are cute, they are doctors, (spelling it out in the air for her slow friend) cute doctors, doctors who are cute!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Hey, c'mon, cut it out.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: She said, 'Cut it out, **get real**', and I did.
expressions.speaker: Chandler (인터넷 친구의 말을 인용)
expressions.nuance: 상대가 허세를 부리거나 비현실적인 태도를 보일 때 직접적으로 촉구하는 말. 약간 도전적인 뉘앙스가 있어 친한 사이에서 더 자연스러움.
expressions.example: 준비도 없이 스타트업을 하겠다는 친구에게 → "Get real — you can't launch a startup with zero savings and zero experience."

occ.speaker: Mr. Tribbiani
occ.text: Gotta go. I miss you too, I love you, but it's getting real late now

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Yeah, I know. He can get really competitive.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: We can't, alright? (To the women) We're sorry. You have no idea how sorry, but... We promised we'd find this monkey. If you see him, he's about yea high and answers to the name Marcel, so if we could get some pictures of you, you'd really be helping us out.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: All right, stop it, you're **freaking me out**.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 예상치 못한 행동이나 말이 심리적 불편함을 줄 때 씀. 'scare'보다 구어적이고, 무서움보다는 낯설거나 이상한 것에 대한 반응에 더 잘 어울림.
expressions.example: 갑자기 말없이 빤히 쳐다보는 사람 → "Stop staring at me like that — you're freaking me out."

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Uh-huh. Okay. Okay, don't you think, maybe, though, it's just that he's so perceptive that it freaks you out?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Dad, before I was born, did you freak out at all?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh, I tasted Ben's milk, and Ross freaked out.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: We're **living in the moment**. God, it is so nice for once to not have to get all hung up on 'Where is this going?'
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 미래 걱정 없이 지금 이 순간에 집중하며 즐기다
expressions.nuance: 관계·여행·일상 등 다양한 맥락에서 씀. 계획이나 결과에 집착하지 않고 현재를 즐기자는 태도를 표현하며, 긍정적이지만 때로는 변화를 회피하는 핑계로도 쓰임.
expressions.example: 연애 초기에 너무 미래를 걱정하는 친구에게 → "Just live in the moment — you don't need to figure everything out on the first date."

expressions.dialogue: Monica: It is so nice for once to not have to get all hung up on '**Where is this going?**'
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 이 관계(또는 상황)가 어디로 향하고 있어?
expressions.nuance: 특히 연애에서 진지한 방향성을 물을 때 씀. Monica처럼 인용 형태로 써서 그 불안감 자체를 묘사하는 방식으로도 자주 등장함.
expressions.example: 몇 달째 만나는 사람에게 대화를 꺼냄 → "I finally had to ask him — where is this going? We've been seeing each other for four months."

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Well, you can't know where I am all the time. Look, this marriage is never gonna work if you don't trust me.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Ross let me ask you a question. All jokes aside, where is this relationship going?

occ.speaker: Mona
occ.text: Y'know, like where are we? Where is this relationship going?

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: Afraid to ask him? / Monica: **Could not be more terrified.**
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 도치된 구조로 감정을 극적으로 강조하는 패턴. 부정적 감정(terrified, nervous)뿐 아니라 긍정적 감정(excited, proud)에도 자유롭게 씀. 짧게 독립절로 쓰는 게 특히 구어적.
expressions.example: 기다리던 여행 전날 밤 → "I couldn't be more excited — I've been looking forward to this for months."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Couldn't be more out. (throws in cards)

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Uh, well yeah-yeah, I've got all of that going on. Yeah, listen uh, I want you to make sure you tell Chandler that he couldn't have been more wrong! Uh-oh! I gotta go Monica, my uh, my sushi's here!

expressions.dialogue: Monica: Hey, you know I got a question for ya. Just a little thing, **no pressure**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 부담 갖지 마, 강요하는 게 아니야
expressions.nuance: 민감한 주제나 부탁을 꺼내기 전에 상대방을 안심시키는 표현. 역설적으로 '이건 사실 중요한 질문이야'라는 신호로 읽힐 수도 있음.
expressions.example: 친구에게 이사 도움을 부탁하며 → "Could you help me move on Saturday? No pressure if you have other plans."

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (gets up) No, no, no, no, no, no pressure, no pressure!

occ.speaker: Frank
occ.text: Yeah, so we just thought we'd stop by and let you know there's still no pressure.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Ross, there is no pressure on you. Okay? I mean you can as involved as you want. (Ross nods.)

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: If you don't meet her now, you're gonna be **kicking yourself** when you're 80 — which is hard to do, and that's how you break a hip.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 기회를 놓쳤을 때의 강렬한 후회를 강조하는 표현. 'you'll kick yourself'처럼 미래형으로 써서 경고하는 패턴이 특히 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 한정판 티켓을 망설이는 친구에게 → "Buy it now — you'll kick yourself if it sells out and you missed it."

expressions.dialogue: Best Man: It's not a lot of women who would've **had the guts** to come back here tonight.
expressions.speaker: Best Man
expressions.nuance: 'courage'보다 구어적이고 강렬한 표현. 쉽지 않은 행동을 해낸 것을 칭찬할 때, 혹은 반어적으로 무모함을 지적할 때도 씀.
expressions.example: 상사에게 직접 반대 의견을 낸 동료를 보고 → "I can't believe you actually had the guts to say that to his face."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No. You decided to go into the out-of-work actor business. Now that wasn't easy, but you did it! And I'd like to believe that when the right woman comes along, you will have the courage and the guts to say "No thanks, I'm married."

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Well, Joshua's coming in tomorrow and since I don't have the guts to ask him out, I'm going to sell him a coat and put this note in the pocket.

expressions.dialogue: Mrs. Wineburg: Well, it's wonderful to have you **up and about** again, dear.
expressions.speaker: Mrs. Wineburg
expressions.meaning: 회복해서 다시 활동하다, 일어나서 정상적으로 돌아다니다
expressions.nuance: 병이나 힘든 일을 겪은 후 회복해 다시 활동 중임을 묘사하는 따뜻한 표현. 어른들이 많이 쓰며 격려와 안도의 뉘앙스가 있음.
expressions.example: 수술 후 빠르게 회복한 친구에게 → "It's so good to see you up and about — I was really worried about you."

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I think we are getting a little ahead of selves here. Okay. Okay. I am just going to get up, go to work and not think about him all day. Or else I'm just gonna get up and go to work.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: We can't tell him, you can't go up to a guy you barely know and talk about his.... stuff.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh wow, I hope you don't take this the wrong way but, I know we had plans to meet up tonight and, ugh, I'm just kinda worried about what it might do to our friendship.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: (at the microphone) I promised myself that I would **make it through** at least one of your weddings.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 힘들거나 어려운 상황을 버텨내다, 끝까지 해내다
expressions.nuance: 'survive'와 비슷하지만 더 구어적. 감정적으로 힘들거나 지루하거나 고통스러운 상황을 끝까지 완수했을 때 씀.
expressions.example: 긴 장례식을 겨우 감정 억누르며 마침 → "I barely made it through the eulogy without crying."

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Well Phoebe, we gotta do something! (They turn the corner.) Well, y'know. I mean there's no way Joey's gonna make it in time. So I'm gonna through the hotel and see if there's any other weddings going on.

expressions.dialogue: Best Man: Now, **in all seriousness**, it's not a lot of women who would've had the guts to come back here tonight.
expressions.speaker: Best Man
expressions.nuance: 가벼운 분위기나 농담 이후에 진지한 발언으로 전환할 때 씀. 'seriously though'와 거의 같지만 약간 더 격식 있는 느낌.
expressions.example: 웃기는 건배사를 치고 나서 진심을 전할 때 → "But in all seriousness — you're one of the most talented people I know."

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: It's like a watched pot, you know — if you keep looking at it then the door is never gonna boil.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 기다린다고 빨리 오는 게 아니다, 조급하게 지켜봐도 더 늦게 느껴진다
expressions.nuance: 기다리는 것을 조급하게 지켜보는 사람에게 쓰는 속담. Phoebe가 '문이 끓는다'고 잘못 적용하는 것이 웃음 포인트이지만, 속담 자체는 그대로 씀.
expressions.example: 답장을 기다리며 폰만 바라보는 친구에게 → "Stop refreshing your inbox every five seconds — a watched pot never boils."

expressions.dialogue: Ross: (seeing the beautiful woman walk in) Yeah, 'cause **life's just that kind**.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 좋은 일이 너무 쉽게 일어날 것 같을 때 내뱉는 냉소적 빈정거림. 뒤에 '(not)'이 생략된 구조로, 'that kind'를 강하게 내뱉으면 빈정거리는 뉘앙스가 살아남.
expressions.example: 우연히 이상형이 바로 옆집으로 이사 옴 → "Oh sure, she's perfect and single and lives next door. Life's just that kind."

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: I can not believe I have to **walk down the aisle** in front of 200 people looking like something you drink when you're nauseous.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 결혼식 본식에서 신부나 들러리가 통로를 걷는 구체적 장면을 묘사하는 동시에, '결혼하다'의 비유적 표현으로도 자주 쓰임.
expressions.example: 오랜 커플이 드디어 결혼 소식을 알렸음 → "After eight years together, she's finally going to walk down the aisle."

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Yes, and I know that you'd say no if he asked you, but I'm sorry; how great would you look walking down the aisle in this Donna Carin. (shows her the picture.)

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I've never walked down the aisle knowing it can't end in divorce.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: On a cliff, in Barbados, at sunset, and Stevie Wonder sang Isn't She Lovely as I walked down the aisle.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Hey, look, Phoebe — I wanted to meet her **in person** too, okay, but she's married.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 온라인·전화·문자가 아닌 실제 만남을 강조할 때 씀. 'face to face'와 거의 같지만 더 일상적. 1990년대 인터넷 초기에 특히 부각된 표현이지만 지금도 그대로 씀.
expressions.example: 온라인으로만 알던 사람을 드디어 만나게 됨 → "We've been talking for months online, but I'm finally going to meet her in person next week."

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: I personally could have a gallon of Alan.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: I don't know, I mean, he's a good person, and he can be really sweet, and in some ways I think he is so right for me, it's just... I hate that guy!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Eight and a half hours of aptitude tests, intelligence tests, personality tests... and what do I learn? (he taps the results and reads them) "You are ideally suited for a career in data processing for a large multinational corporation."

expressions.expression: I'm afraid I might just be
expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: Are you the cutest? / Chandler: **I'm afraid I might just be.**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 그런 것 같기도 해 (겸손한 척하며 인정하는 유머)
expressions.nuance: 'I'm afraid'(유감이지만)와 'might just'(아마도)를 결합해, 칭찬을 우쭐하지 않은 척하면서 수락하는 익살스러운 패턴. 진지한 상황에서 쓰면 어색함.
expressions.example: 친구들이 내가 파티에서 제일 재미있었다고 함 → "I'm afraid I might just be the life of every party."

expressions.dialogue: Richard: Monica, I don't wanna lose you, so if I have to do it all over again, then I will. / Monica: And if you hadn't said '**if I have to**' like seventeen times, then I'd be saying 'okay, let's do it.'
expressions.speaker: Richard / Monica
expressions.nuance: 진심으로 원하지 않지만 상황상 받아들이겠다는 뜻을 드러냄. Monica의 대사처럼, 이 표현을 반복하면 상대에게 마지못해 동의한다는 신호를 보낼 수 있으니 주의.
expressions.example: 원하지 않는 야근을 억지로 수락하며 → "I'll stay and finish the report if I have to, but I really hope it can wait until tomorrow."

occ.speaker: Paul
occ.text: I know, I know, I'm such an idiot. I guess I should have caught on when she started going to the dentist four and five times a week. I mean, how clean can teeth get?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I think for us, kissing is pretty much like an opening act, y'know? I mean it's like the stand-up comedian you have to sit through before Pink Floyd comes out.

occ.speaker: Lizzie
occ.text: No, no, I ha-I have to give you something.

expressions.expression: I guess we just keep dancing
expressions.dialogue: Monica: So what now? / Richard: **I guess we just keep dancing.**
expressions.speaker: Richard
expressions.meaning: 딱히 해결책 없이 그냥 지금처럼 계속 가는 거지
expressions.nuance: 어려운 결정이나 불확실한 상황에서 결론 없이 현상을 유지하는 것을 비유적으로 표현. 실제 춤을 추는 장면에서 관계의 메타포로 이중 의미로 쓰인 대사이며, 'keep + -ing' 패턴 자체도 '계속 ~하다'로 폭넓게 씀.
expressions.example: 팀이 방향을 정하지 못하고 회의가 끝남 → "We don't have an answer yet — I guess we just keep dancing until something becomes clear."
